claim: In non-small cell lung cancer, PD-L1 functions as a tumor-expressed ligand of the PD-1 immune checkpoint.
question: Does PD-L1 act as a tumor-expressed ligand of the PD-1 immune checkpoint in NSCLC?

claim: PD-L1 on tumor cells and some immune cells binds PD-1 on recently activated T cells.
question: Does PD-L1 on tumor cells and some immune cells bind PD-1 on recently activated T cells?

claim: Binding of PD-L1 to PD-1 on recently activated T cells suppresses adaptive immune functions, including blocking cytotoxic T lymphocyte responses, reducing T and B cell proliferation, and decreasing cytokine production.
question: Does PD-L1 binding to PD-1 on activated T cells suppress adaptive immune functions?

claim: In non-small cell lung cancer, PD-L1 (the PD-1/PD-L1 axis) is a therapeutic target and is targeted in immune checkpoint therapies, including in combination with CTLA-4 blockade.
question: Is the PD-1/PD-L1 axis a therapeutic target in NSCLC, including in combination with CTLA-4 blockade?

claim: In advanced non-small cell lung cancer, tumor PD-L1 expression is used as the preferred biomarker to predict response to PD-1/PD-L1 inhibitors, and patients with high PD-L1 expression derive greater benefit from single-agent anti-PD-1/PD-L1 therapies.
question: In advanced NSCLC, is tumor PD-L1 expression the preferred biomarker predicting greater benefit from single-agent PD-1/PD-L1 therapy?
answer_note: Advanced disease or single-agent context not specified.

claim: In NSCLC patients with high tumor mutational burden, first-line nivolumab plus ipilimumab significantly prolonged progression-free survival regardless of PD-L1 expression status (CheckMate-227).
question: In NSCLC with high tumor mutational burden, did first-line nivolumab plus ipilimumab prolong progression-free survival regardless of PD-L1 status in CheckMate-227?

claim: Elevated PD-L1 expression in many NSCLC tumors promotes immune escape.
question: Does elevated PD-L1 expression in many NSCLC tumors promote immune escape?

claim: Elevated PD-L1 expression in many NSCLC tumors is associated with shorter survival.
question: Is elevated PD-L1 expression in many NSCLC tumors associated with shorter survival?
